name: OptionsManager
summary: The OptionsManager class manages various kinds of OptionsProviders on a Model. To get the OptionsProvider key list, run the following code in the ruby console:

methods.summary: The [] method is used to get an option provider by name or index.
methods.remarks: The [] method is used to get an option provider by name or index. For example, to get the UnitsOptions on the Model, you could use the command:

params.name: index
params.doc: The index of the OptionsProvider object.

returns.doc: optionprovider - an OptionsProvider object if successful, otherwise nil.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length. Returns integer - number of OptionsProvider objects if successful.

returns.doc: integer - number of OptionsProvider objects if successful

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through options providers.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through options providers.

methods.name: keys
methods.summary: The keys method is used to get a list of keys in the OptionsManager.
methods.remarks: The keys method is used to get a list of keys in the OptionsManager.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is an alias of #size.
methods.remarks: The #length method is an alias of #size.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method returns the number of Sketchup::OptionsProvider objects inside this Sketchup::OptionsManager.
methods.remarks: The #size method returns the number of Sketchup::OptionsProvider objects inside this Sketchup::OptionsManager.
